zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Chesney於大約1160年出生在英國諾福爾可時，她的父親William de Chesney46歲，母親Albreda  Aubreye de Poynings25歲。她於1189年之前與Hugh de Cressy結婚。她去世於1231年1月7日在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德瓦可沃斯享年72歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Chesney于大约1160年出生在英国諾福爾可时，她的父亲William de Chesney46岁，母亲Albreda  Aubreye de Poynings25岁。她于1189年之前与Hugh de Cressy结婚。她去世于1231年1月7日在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德瓦可沃斯享年72岁。

nameMeanings.name: Chesney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Antrim, Down): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (an) tSasanaigh, see McChesney .
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Chaney  , a cognate of 3 below.
French: topographic name for someone who lived by or in an oak wood, from Old French chesnai ‘oak grove’ (from chesne ‘oak’). Compare Cheney 2.

variants.name: McChesney
meanings: Scottish (Galloway) and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (an) tSasanaigh ‘son of the Saxon (i.e. the Englishman)’. The spelling seems to represent Mac Shasanaigh, to be pronounced originally as McHasney, rather than the English spelling ch.

variants.name: Chestnut
meanings: Scottish (Renfrewshire and Lanarkshire) and northern Irish: altered form of Chesney , shortened from McChesney . Its assimilation to the English word chestnut is due to folk etymology, the word itself being first recorded in 1519. The surname was probably taken to Ireland at the time of the 17th-century Plantation of Ulster.
English (Norfolk): probably a variant of Chestney (see Chaney ), altered by folk etymological association with the word chestnut.

variants.name: Chesna
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Chesney  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Chesney was born about 1160, in Norfolk, England, her father, William de Chesney, was 46 and her mother, Albreda  Aubreye de Poynings, was 25. She married Hugh de Cressy before 1189. She died on 7 January 1231, in Warkworth, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Chesney est née environ 1160, à Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, William de Chesney, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Albreda  Aubreye de Poynings, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh de Cressy avant 1189. Elle est décédée le 7 janvier 1231, à Warkworth, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 72 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Chesney um 1160 in Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Chesney 46 und ihre Mutter Albreda  Aubreye de Poynings 25. Sie hat vor 1189 Hugh de Cressy geheiratet. Sie starb am 7. Januar 1231 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Warkworth, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

Lei sposato Hugh de Cressy prima del 1189. Morì il 7 gennaio 1231, nel Warkworth, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 72 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Chesney(이)가 대략 1160년 영국 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Chesney(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Albreda  Aubreye de Poynings(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1189년 이전 Hugh de Cressy(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1231년 1월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 워k워스에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Chesney nasceu aproximadamente 1160, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Chesney, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Albreda  Aubreye de Poynings, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Hugh de Cressy antes 1189. Ela faleceu em 7 de janeiro de 1231, em Warkworth, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 72 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Chesney родилась около 1160 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Chesney было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Albreda  Aubreye de Poynings, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh de Cressy перед 1189 г. Она умерла 7 января 1231 г. в местности варкворт, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 72 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Chesney nació aproximadamente 1160, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Chesney, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Albreda  Aubreye de Poynings, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Hugh de Cressy antes de 1189. Murió el 7 de enero de 1231, en Warkworth, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 72 años.
